qas.a:
Unit Commanders, ADPMs, and AM personnel have the authority to revoke airfield driving privileges
Drivers must be knowledgeable of and comply with the procedures outlined in AFI 13-213
At least a SSgt/7 level or above, or civilian equivalent

Ensure the unit ADPMs perform a program assessment semiannually
Ensures second time airfield test failures receive additional training from unit ADPM
Notify unit ADPM of individuals whose driving privileges have been suspended/revoked

qas.q: Who signs the Memorandum for Record?
qas.a: Unit Commander

Maintains semiannual meeting minutes
Assists unit ADPMs in ensuring TDY and/or contract personnel obtain appropriate training

qas.q: Who maintains the Airfield Drivers Information File (ADIF)?
qas.a: Wing ADPM

qas.q: Airfield Drivers Information File is used for...?
qas.a: Passing important safety messages regarding airfield driving
Coordinates with AM to schedule personnel for the Airfield Driving Final Exam

Issues the driving final exam and approves af form 483 upon completion
Conducts quality control measures to monitor effectiveness of unit Airfield driver training programs

qas.q: Air Force Runway Safety Action Team (AFRSAT)
qas.a: Help the unit develop a plan to correct the negative trend or unsafe condition within 15 days of the visit
Initiating training and ensuring all criteria has been met before coordinating the final exams with AM

Unit ADPMs will send individuals with AM with a pre filled AF Form 483
AM will approve/sign

qas.q: Who may suspend or revoke an airfield POV pass for airfield driving violations?
qas.a: Airfield Management (AM)
The INST critical area, RWYs, and overruns to include an area no less than 100 feet extending out from each side of the RWYs and overruns
Commanders and Unit ADPMs may only revoke privileges for personnel assigned to their unit and will notify the wing ADPM when doing so

Within 25 ft of aircraft: 5 mph
Towing 1 or more than 1 piece of maintenance equipment: 5 mph
Towing a single piece of maintenance equipment: 10 mph
AGE: 15
Limited visibility: 5

High intensity yellow beacons, 4 way hazards must be installed
Red beacons used for emergencies
Automatic daytime running lights must be aware of systems operating procedures
AM will relay limited visibility when in effect to the tower net and landline MOC
CMA or any TWY
Fire bottle, maintenance stands, AGE and vehicles
Used for communicating with aircraft or ground vehicles if their radios stop working
